message:
feat(ops): add policy metrics aggregation and health dashboard

PolicyMetricsCollector aggregates subsystem metrics into unified dashboard
with health indicators (denial rate, quarantine density, violations,
audit chain integrity, kill switch state). Includes MetricTimeSeries
for trending. 24 unit tests.

Precursor for ft-3681t.7.1/ft-3681t.7.2 observability and dashboards.

Bead: ft-2hfup

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"ft-24l8","title":"[EPIC] FTUI-07 Test, Quality, and CI Gate Migration","description":"## Purpose
Establish migration-specific test gates, artifact standards, and CI enforcement.

## Why
A UI migration without strict quality gates will drift and become impossible to trust.

## Focus
- unit and snapshot coverage for ftui views
- PTY E2E for lifecycle/input/resize/log stress
- perf budgets and regression gates
- docs-smoke and schema-level drift checks","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-08T20:07:23.549109561Z","created_by":"GrayHarbor","updated_at":"2026-02-09T04:13:34.117308952Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T04:13:34.117246075Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-24l8","depends_on_id":"ft-2wed","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-24l8","depends_on_id":"ft-2zd7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-24l8","depends_on_id":"ft-38vw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-24shg","title":"Integrate rano — network attribution subprocess bridge","description":"## rano Integration (Wave 1)

Create `network_observer.rs` behind `network-observer` feature gate.

### What to Build
1. `network_observer.rs`: SubprocessBridge wrapping the `rano` CLI
2. Functions:
   - attribute_connection(remote_addr: &str) -> NetworkAttribution
   - check_connectivity() -> ConnectivityStatus
3. Types: NetworkAttribution { provider, region, latency_ms, is_trusted }
4. Backpressure mapping: high latency or unreachable → Yellow/Red tier
5. Feature gate: `network-observer`

### Unit Tests (30+)
- test_attribution_parses_provider
- test_attribution_parses_region
- test_connectivity_check_passes
- test_connectivity_check_fails_gracefully
- test_backpressure_green_low_latency
- test_backpressure_yellow_high_latency
- test_fail_open_when_rano_unavailable
- (+ 23 more)

### Logging
- debug!(bridge = "rano", remote = %addr, provider = %prov, latency_ms = %lat)","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"IndigoReef","estimated_minutes":180,"created_at":"2026-02-22T07:25:45.499643Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-22T08:01:53.376850Z","closed_at":"2026-02-22T08:01:53.376804Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["integration","network","subprocess"]}

 {"id":"ft-2h2hp","title":"[policy] Composable rule predicate AST for policy DSL","description":"Implement a composable RulePredicate enum with And/Or/Not combinators for policy rule matching. Currently PolicyRuleMatch only supports flat AND/OR within categories. This adds a recursive boolean predicate tree that can express complex policy logic. Types: RulePredicate, PredicateEvaluator, and serde support. Includes comprehensive unit tests and proptest coverage. Support bead for ft-3681t.6.1.","notes":"Implemented policy_dsl.rs (~750 lines): RulePredicate AST with And/Or/Not, 9 atomic matchers (action/actor/surface/pane_id/title/cwd/domain/command/agent), DslRule evaluator with priority/severity/specificity, PredicateTrace for audit, message interpolation, telemetry. 47 unit tests + 36 proptests. Commit 865e91fd.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PinkForge","created_at":"2026-03-09T22:14:35.889443Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T22:27:13.135951Z","closed_at":"2026-03-09T22:24:03.825410Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dsl","governance","policy"],"comments":[{"id":2062,"issue_id":"ft-2h2hp","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"PinkForge completed: RulePredicate enum with 14 variants (Action/Actor/Surface/PaneId/PaneTitle/PaneCwd/PaneDomain/CommandPattern/AgentType/And/Or/Not/True/False), evaluate(), from_flat_match() converter, 23 unit tests + 22 property-based tests (boolean algebra laws). Commit c5d58c72.","created_at":"2026-03-09T22:27:13Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-2h5wv","title":"Refactor MuxPool (mux_pool.rs) to asupersync","description":"# Refactor MuxPool (mux_pool.rs) to asupersync

## Background
mux_pool.rs (583 LOC) wraps generic Pool for DirectMuxClient connections with WezTerm-specific health checks.

## Dependencies
- Pool refactoring (wa-36idf) must be done first
- DirectMuxClient refactoring (wa-p48pw) must be done first

## Migration
1. Thread Cx through to Pool methods
2. Update health check (list_panes()) to use Cx
3. Update connection factory (DirectMuxClient::connect) to use Cx
4. Ensure pool config works with Budget model

## Comprehensive unit tests (LabRuntime)
1. **Basic lifecycle**: create pool → acquire → use → release → shutdown
2. **Health check integration**: mock unhealthy client, verify pool replaces it
3. **Connection factory**: verify DirectMuxClient::connect called with correct Cx
4. **Idle eviction**: connections idle past timeout are evicted (virtual time)
5. **Concurrent acquire**: N tasks acquire simultaneously, max_size respected
6. **Acquire timeout**: full pool, Budget::deadline fires, clean Cancelled outcome
7. **Pool drain on shutdown**: scope exit waits for all connections returned
8. **Stats accuracy**: verify all counters under concurrent load

Each test logs: pool stats at each step, health check results, connection create/destroy events.

## Acceptance criteria
- MuxPool works with asupersync DirectMuxClient and Pool
- Health checks work through Cx
- Idle eviction works with virtual time
- 8+ LabRuntime tests
- Obligation leak oracle passes

## LabRuntime DPOR
- **Pool concurrency testing with DPOR**: Use LabRuntime schedule exploration (DPOR) to exhaustively test concurrent MuxPool operations:
  - Multiple tasks acquiring connections while health checks run — verify no stale connections returned
  - Interleaved acquire + release + eviction — verify pool invariants (active + idle <= max_size)
  - Concurrent shutdown while tasks hold connections — verify clean drain without orphaned connections
  - Race between health check failure and acquire — verify correct fallback to new connection creation

## Benchmark requirements
- **Criterion benchmarks for pool scaling with connection count**: Add `benches/mux_pool_scaling.rs` measuring:
  - Acquire + release cycle latency as a function of pool size (max_size=1,2,4,8,16,32)
  - Health check overhead per acquire operation
  - Pool scaling: throughput (ops/sec) with increasing concurrent task count
  - Idle eviction scan time as a function of pool size
  - Connection factory overhead (DirectMuxClient::connect latency)

## Test and Observability Plan
1. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy path, boundary/edge cases, and explicit failure/error behavior for logic introduced or changed by this bead.
2. Add deterministic integration and end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise full workflow behavior, including at least one negative/failure scenario and one recovery scenario.
3. Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs (timestamp, subsystem, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions, outcomes, reason_code/error_code).
4. Persist triage artifacts for failed runs (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, and concise failure summaries) so regressions can be root-caused quickly.
5. Ensure test commands and evidence locations are documented in the bead implementation notes and wired into normal validation/CI paths.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Migrate MuxPool async behavior to asupersync while preserving pool correctness, health-check semantics, and acquisition/release guarantees.
2. Validate dependency alignment with DirectMuxClient/pool foundation beads and maintain deterministic behavior under contention and unhealthy-client replacement.
3. Require comprehensive validation: unit tests, integration tests, and end-to-end scripts for lifecycle, health-check replacement, backpressure behavior, and timeout/cancellation flows.
4. Require structured diagnostics/logging for pool state transitions (acquire/release/replace/evict), health-check outcomes, and latency metrics.
5. Closure requires rch-offloaded validation evidence and explicit residual-risk notes.","notes":"2026-03-01 AmberDesert progress: added explicit Cx-threaded APIs for pool and mux pool migration boundary.\n\nCode changes:\n- crates/frankenterm-core/src/pool.rs: add try_acquire_with_cx/acquire_with_cx, keep existing acquire/try_acquire as wrappers via cx::for_testing().\n- crates/frankenterm-core/src/vendored/mux_pool.rs: add list_panes_with_cx/get_lines_with_cx/get_pane_render_changes_with_cx/get_pane_render_changes_batch_with_cx/write_to_pane_with_cx/send_paste_with_cx/health_check_with_cx; migrate internal acquire/recovery flow to *_with_cx path and make legacy methods wrappers.\n- Added focused tests for new Cx surfaces in both files.\n\nValidation:\n- rustfmt --edition 2024 on changed files: PASS.\n- rch exec -- env CARGO_HOME=/Users/jemanuel/projects/frankenterm/.cargo-amber2 CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-amberdesert2 cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib : PASS (finished dev profile in 2m56s).\n- rch test/check paths for test targets currently unstable due remote worker connectivity failures + long local fallback compiles; timeout hit on bounded test runs.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T03:50:21.992539Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-09T01:45:53.097775Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","quality-gate","sequencing","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-2h5wv","depends_on_id":"ft-36idf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-2h5wv","depends_on_id":"ft-p48pw","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":429,"issue_id":"ft-2h5wv","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"SapphireMoose progress slice (2026-02-20): Updated crates/frankenterm-core/src/vendored/mux_pool.rs mock servers to handle WriteToPane/SendPaste PDUs and added tests pool_write_to_pane_succeeds + pool_send_paste_reuses_connection. Validation via rch exec (workers unreachable -> local fallback): cargo test -p frankenterm-core --features vendored,asupersync-runtime --lib pool_write_to_pane_succeeds -- --nocapture PASS; cargo test -p frankenterm-core --features vendored,asupersync-runtime --lib pool_send_paste_reuses_connection -- --nocapture PASS. During validation found unrelated unit-test compile blocker in crates/frankenterm-core/src/cx.rs spawn_bounded_empty_tasks (type inference with empty Vec under this config); applied minimal test-only type annotation so vendored lib tests compile.","created_at":"2026-02-20T08:14:36Z"},{"id":430,"issue_id":"ft-2h5wv","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Additional validation slice: rch exec (local fallback due worker outage) cargo test -p frankenterm-core --features vendored,asupersync-runtime --lib vendored::mux_pool::tests:: -- --nocapture => 41 passed / 0 failed. Also ran rch exec cargo check -p frankenterm-core --features vendored,asupersync-runtime --lib => PASS (warnings only). Workspace-wide clippy -D warnings remains blocked by unrelated pre-existing issues outside mux_pool scope (rangeset/uds/blob-leases/pty and additional integration-test compile drift).","created_at":"2026-02-20T08:17:26Z"},{"id":431,"issue_id":"ft-2h5wv","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Benchmark slice added: crates/frankenterm-core/benches/mux_pool_scaling.rs + Cargo.toml bench registration (required-features=[vendored]). Scenarios implemented: acquire/release cycle latency across max_size {1,2,4,8,16,32}; health_check overhead; throughput scaling across concurrency {1,2,4,8,16,32}; idle eviction scan by pool size; DirectMuxClient::connect overhead. Bench file rustfmt check passed. Targeted bench compile command via rch exec (cargo check -p frankenterm-core --features vendored --bench mux_pool_scaling) blocked by unrelated pre-existing compile errors in crates/frankenterm-core/src/fibonacci_heap.rs.","created_at":"2026-02-20T08:20:28Z"},{"id":432,"issue_id":"ft-2h5wv","author":"TurquoiseGlacier","text":"TurquoiseGlacier slice (2026-02-22): migrated crates/frankenterm-core/src/vendored/mux_pool.rs test harness off tokio test/runtime bindings for this module. Changes: (1) replaced all remaining #[tokio::test] in mux_pool.rs with runtime-compat runner style via new local run_async_test helper (RuntimeBuilder::current_thread().build() + block_on), (2) switched fallback unix_stream_read import from tokio::io::AsyncReadExt to crate::runtime_compat::unix::AsyncReadExt, (3) added CompatRuntime trait import required for block_on method resolution. Validation via rch exec: env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-turquoise-ft2h5wv CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 cargo test -p frankenterm-core --features vendored --lib vendored::mux_pool::tests:: -- --nocapture => PASS (41 passed, 0 failed). Additional asupersync check attempt via rch exec: env CARGO_TARGET_DIR=target-turquoise-ft2h5wv CARGO_BUILD_JOBS=1 cargo check -p frankenterm-core --features vendored,asupersync-runtime --lib remains blocked by pre-existing runtime_compat select! gating errors in snapshot_engine/runtime_compat (E0433), but mux_pool.rs no longer emits the prior block_on trait-scope error after this slice.","created_at":"2026-02-22T00:30:50Z"},{"id":433,"issue_id":"ft-2h5wv","author":"TurquoiseGlacier","text":"Correction/clarification: with current branch HEAD in this shared workspace, the net code delta from my slice is one-line import update in mux_pool tests (use crate::runtime_compat::{CompatRuntime, task, timeout};). The runtime-compat test wrappers and unix read helper imports are already present in HEAD at this point. This import is required so runtime.block_on(...) resolves via the CompatRuntime trait under the existing test harness.","created_at":"2026-02-22T00:45:20Z"},{"id":2008,"issue_id":"ft-2h5wv","author":"PlumDeer","text":"Reopened on 2026-03-08 after bv --robot-alerts flagged this issue as stale in_progress with 7+ days of inactivity and no recent bead-thread progress. Clearing the stale claim so it can be triaged again by active agents.","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:01:26Z"},{"id":2031,"issue_id":"ft-2h5wv","author":"SandyCliff","text":"SandyCliff progress slice (2026-03-09): added explicit-Cx coverage in crates/frankenterm-core/src/vendored/mux_pool.rs for get_lines_with_cx, get_pane_render_changes_with_cx, write_to_pane_with_cx, send_paste_with_cx, and get_pane_render_changes_batch_with_cx. Also extended the local mux_pool mock server to answer GetLines requests so those explicit-Cx tests can execute against the same socket harness. Formatting: rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/frankenterm-core/src/vendored/mux_pool.rs PASS. Offloaded validation attempts via rch exec selected workers but both failed remote SSH execution and fell back to local cargo; those local fallbacks were terminated to honor the repo offload-only policy, so cargo check/test/clippy evidence remains infra-blocked rather than complete.","created_at":"2026-03-09T01:42:13Z"},{"id":2032,"issue_id":"ft-2h5wv","author":"SandyCliff","text":"Correction from SandyCliff (2026-03-09): after checking HEAD/blame more carefully, the explicit-Cx mux_pool tests and GetLines mock-server support I thought I was adding are already present in commit 8132708e (2026-03-08). My local edit attempt was a no-op and produced no net source diff. The only durable output from that pass was validation/infra investigation: rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/frankenterm-core/src/vendored/mux_pool.rs passes; rch offload attempts still fail remote SSH and fall back local, so cargo validation remains infra-blocked. I am pivoting to a different non-overlapping slice rather than claiming duplicate work.","created_at":"2026-03-09T01:43:46Z"},{"id":2033,"issue_id":"ft-2h5wv","author":"SandyCliff","text":"SandyCliff progress slice (2026-03-09): updated crates/frankenterm-core/benches/mux_pool_scaling.rs to add explicit-Cx benchmark coverage under asupersync-runtime. Added a compat runtime helper plus two new benchmark lanes: health_check_overhead/with_cx (benching MuxPool::health_check_with_cx) and connection_factory_overhead/with_cx (benching DirectMuxClient::connect_with_cx). Also added matching BenchBudget entries so these explicit-Cx paths emit artifact metadata alongside the existing ambient lanes. Local formatting verification passed: rustfmt --edition 2024 crates/frankenterm-core/benches/mux_pool_scaling.rs && rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/frankenterm-core/benches/mux_pool_scaling.rs. Targeted offloaded validation attempted twice via rch exec for cargo check -p frankenterm-core --bench mux_pool_scaling --features vendored,asupersync-runtime, on workers vmi1153651 and vmi1227854; both remote executions failed SSH connection and fell back to local cargo, and those local fallbacks were terminated immediately to honor the repo offload-only policy. So compile/test evidence remains infra-blocked, not complete.","created_at":"2026-03-09T01:45:53Z"}]}

 {"id":"ft-2hnp","title":"Implement Slack/Discord/email/webhook senders","description":"## What
Implement channel-specific senders using the unified notification interface.

## Why
Operators rely on existing collaboration tools; we should reuse existing webhook templates and add email without duplicating logic.

## How
- Slack/Discord: reuse existing webhook template renderers
- Generic webhook: existing path + new interface
- Email: add SMTP sender with redacted summaries

## Success Criteria
- Each sender validates config and handles failures gracefully
- Payloads include redacted summaries and actionable links","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:08:08.242237143Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-11T06:34:51.429300Z","closed_at":"2026-02-11T06:34:51.429308Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-2hnp","depends_on_id":"ft-ugaj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:27Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
